content.content:
1→# Kapow — College Power Search: Product Specification
     2→
     3→## 1. Product Overview
     4→
     5→**Kapow (College Power Search)** is a web application that provides a comprehensive, faceted search engine for US colleges and universities. It aggregates data from 12 independent sources into a single searchable interface, enabling users to filter, sort, compare, and export institution data across 48 searchable dimensions.
     6→
     7→### Target Users
     8→
     9→- **Prospective students** researching colleges that fit their academic, financial, geographic, and lifestyle preferences
    10→- **Parents and families** comparing cost, outcomes, and value across institutions
    11→- **High school counselors** building filtered shortlists and exporting data for students
    12→- **AI assistants** querying college data programmatically via the MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint
    13→
    14→### Core Value Proposition
    15→
    16→College Power Search consolidates data that is otherwise scattered across dozens of websites — federal statistics, multiple ranking publications, athletic conferences, climate data, partisan lean scores, and application platform listings — into a single interface with real-time faceted filtering. Users can combine any number of criteria simultaneously, view results in four different formats, create custom ranking blends from multiple sources, and export their findings as CSV or PDF.
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## 2. Feature Inventory
    21→
    22→### 2.1 Faceted Search Engine
    23→
    24→The search engine exposes 48 filterable fields organized into 11 active themes (with 7 additional theme slots reserved for future expansion). Every filter is applied server-side as a parameterized SQL WHERE clause against a denormalized materialized view (`dataset_merged`), ensuring fast queries across the full dataset.
    25→
    26→**Active themes and their fields:**
    27→
    28→| Theme | Fields | Description |
    29→|---|---|---|
    30→| Core | Institution Name (full-text), Unit ID | Name search across institution name, city, and state |
    31→| Location | City, State, Geographic Proximity, Locale, Region, Metro Area, Metro Distance | Where the school is and what kind of setting it occupies |
    32→| Academics | Institution Type, Highest Degree, Enrollment, Graduate Enrollment, Student-Faculty Ratio, Full-Time Faculty Rate, Gender (coed/single-sex), Online Only, Carnegie Classification | Academic profile and size |
    33→| Cost & Financial Aid | Avg Net Price, In-State Tuition, Out-of-State Tuition, Room & Board, Net Price (low-income), Pell Grant Rate, Federal Loan Rate, Endowment | What it costs and how it is funded |
    34→| Outcomes | Admission Rate, Graduation Rate, Retention Rate, Transfer Rate | Student success metrics |
    35→| Rankings | US News National, US News Liberal Arts, Forbes, Washington Monthly National, Washington Monthly Liberal Arts, Niche, Money Score | Rankings from 6 publications (7 lists) |
    36→| Admissions | Application Platforms (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | How to apply |
    37→| Athletics | NCAA Division, NCAA Conference | Athletic conference and competition level |
    38→| Fields of Study | CIP-coded program offerings | What majors and programs are available |
    39→| Cultural Environment | Partisan Direction, Partisan Score, Congressional District | Political leaning of the surrounding area |
    40→| Weather | Koppen Climate Group, Koppen Temperature Subtype, Koppen Precipitation Subtype | Climate classification of the campus location |
    41→
    42→**Filter types supported:**
    43→
    44→| Filter Type | Behavior | Example |
    45→|---|---|---|
    46→| Full-text search | PostgreSQL `tsvector` matching | "MIT" matches Massachusetts Institute of Technology |
    47→| Substring match | Case-insensitive partial match | "Arbor" matches Ann Arbor |
    48→| Single enum | Exact match from a fixed option list | Institution Type = "Public" |
    49→| Multi enum | Match any or all from a set | State in [CA, NY, MA] |
    50→| Numeric range | Min/max bounds on a numeric value | Tuition between $5,000 and $20,000 |
    51→| Exact string | Case-insensitive exact match | Metro Area = "Boston" |
    52→| Exact numeric | Exact integer match | Carnegie Classification = 15 |
    53→| Geographic proximity | PostGIS radius search | Within 50 miles of a given lat/lng |
    54→| Ranking | Numeric range on ranking position | US News rank 1-25 |
    55→
    56→### 2.2 Four Result Views
    57→
    58→Users can switch between four ways to view search results:
    59→
    60→| View | Description |
    61→|---|---|
    62→| **Card** (default) | Responsive grid of summary cards showing key stats per institution |
    63→| **List** | Compact row-based list emphasizing quick scanning |
    64→| **Table** | Full data table with sortable columns, customizable column visibility, and drag-to-reorder |
    65→| **Map** | Leaflet-based map with institution markers; supports click-to-detail |
    66→
    67→The selected view mode persists in `localStorage` and can also be set via URL parameter (`?view=table`).
    68→
    69→### 2.3 Custom Ranking Blends
    70→
    71→Users can create weighted composite rankings by assigning weights to any combination of the 7 ranking fields. When a blend is active, results are sorted by the computed blend score rather than by a single column. Blend weights are serialized in the URL (`?blend=...`) so blended searches can be shared via link.
    72→
    73→### 2.4 Sorting and Pagination
    74→
    75→- Any field marked as `sortable` can be used as the sort column (ascending or descending)
    76→- When a user location is set, results can be sorted by distance
    77→- Pagination supports configurable page sizes (5, 10, 20, 50, 100) with a maximum of 100 results per search
    78→- All sort and pagination state is encoded in URL parameters for shareability
    79→
    80→### 2.5 Column Customization (Table View)
    81→
    82→In table view, users can:
    83→- Show or hide any output field using a column picker
    84→- Reorder columns via drag-and-drop or up/down controls
    85→- Column visibility and order persist in `localStorage`
    86→- Default-visible columns are determined by field metadata from the API
    87→
    88→### 2.6 Institution Detail
    89→
    90→Clicking any institution in any view opens a detail drawer/modal showing expanded information including:
    91→- Core identity (name, city, state, coordinates)
    92→- Institution type and enrollment
    93→- Admission and graduation rates
    94→- Tuition (in-state and out-of-state) and average net price
    95→- Retention rate and student-faculty ratio
    96→
    97→### 2.7 Similar Institutions
    98→
    99→The API provides a `similarInstitutions` query that, given a specific institution, returns other schools with similar profiles based on type, enrollment, admission rate, graduation rate, and tuition. Results include a similarity distance score.
   100→
   101→### 2.8 CSV and PDF Export
   102→
   103→- **CSV Export**: Server-side export via `/api/export/csv` that respects active filters, sort order, and user location. Downloads directly as a file. Includes all exportable fields.
   104→- **PDF Export**: Client-side PDF generation using the current result set, active output fields, and active filter labels. Includes data source attribution.
   105→
   106→### 2.9 Geographic Features
   107→
   108→- **Proximity search**: PostGIS-powered `ST_DWithin` queries with configurable radius (up to ~310 miles / 500 km)
   109→- **Distance calculation**: When a user location is set, each result includes its distance from that location
   110→- **My Price**: When a user's home state is known, the system returns in-state tuition for public schools in that state and out-of-state tuition otherwise
   111→- **Map view**: Interactive Leaflet map with markers for all results in the current page
   112→- **ZIP code resolution**: The `resolveZip` query resolves a US ZIP code to latitude/longitude and state, used by the `LocationInput` component for location-by-ZIP entry (queries `location.zip_coordinates`)
   113→
   114→### 2.10 Authentication and User Preferences
   115→
   116→- **Authentication**: Supabase-based authentication supporting Google, GitHub, and email sign-in via OAuth
   117→- **User profile**: The `me` query returns the authenticated user's ID, email, display name, avatar, and auth provider
   118→- **Preferences**: Authenticated users can save a home location (latitude, longitude, state) that automatically populates the user location for distance calculations and My Price computation
   119→
   120→### 2.11 Sample Searches
   121→
   122→The home page features a carousel of pre-built sample searches that demonstrate the system's filtering capabilities:
   123→
   124→| Sample | Filters Applied |
   125→|---|---|
   126→| Top 25 National | US News National rank <= 25 |
   127→| Affordable in California | State = CA, net price <= $20,000 |
   128→| D1 Small Schools | NCAA Division I, enrollment <= 5,000 |
   129→| Selective & Affordable | Admission rate <= 30%, net price <= $20,000 |
   130→| D3 New England | NCAA Division III, New England region |
   131→| Sunny & Affordable | Dry (Koppen B) climate, net price <= $15,000 |
   132→
   133→Each sample search shows a live preview of matching institutions before the user navigates to the full search page.
   134→
   135→### 2.12 MCP (Model Context Protocol) Endpoint
   136→
   137→The application exposes a WebMCP interface that allows AI clients (such as Claude) to programmatically search the college database. Two tools are registered:
   138→
   139→| Tool | Description |
   140→|---|---|
   141→| `search_colleges` | Execute a filtered search with field/value pairs, limit, and offset |
   142→| `get_search_fields` | Retrieve the full field descriptor catalog including types and options |
   143→
   144→### 2.13 Data Source Attribution
   145→
   146→Every search field is linked to its originating data source. The UI can display source badges and tooltips, and the `dataSources` GraphQL query returns the full catalog with names, short names, URLs, and descriptions. This attribution carries through to PDF exports.
   147→
   148→---
   149→
   150→## 3. Pages
   151→
   152→| Route | Page | Description |
   153→|---|---|---|
   154→| `/` | Home | Landing page with hero search form (name, state, ranking, major), sample search carousel, and feature highlights |
   155→| `/search` | Search | Main search interface with collapsible filter sidebar (desktop: fixed 280px sidebar; mobile: toggle-to-show), results area with view mode toggle, export buttons, pagination, and institution detail drawer |
   156→| `/about` | About | Information page about the application |
   157→| `/auth/callback` | Auth Callback | OAuth redirect handler for Supabase authentication |
   158→
   159→All pages share a common `Header` (with navigation and auth controls) and `Footer`.
   160→
   161→---
   162→
   163→## 4. User Stories
   164→
   165→### Student
   166→
   167→- As a student, I want to search for colleges by name so I can quickly find a specific school.
   168→- As a student, I want to filter by state, cost, and admission rate simultaneously so I can find affordable schools I have a realistic chance of attending.
   169→- As a student, I want to filter by fields of study so I can find schools that offer my intended major.
   170→- As a student, I want to see how far each school is from my home so I can gauge travel time.
   171→- As a student, I want to view results on a map so I can see the geographic distribution of matching schools.
   172→- As a student, I want to create a custom ranking blend weighting cost and graduation rate so I can find the best value schools by my own criteria.
   173→
   174→### Parent
   175→
   176→- As a parent, I want to compare in-state and out-of-state tuition so I can understand the true cost.
   177→- As a parent, I want to see my personalized net price (My Price) so I know what public schools in my state would actually cost.
   178→- As a parent, I want to sort by graduation rate so I can prioritize schools where students succeed.
   179→- As a parent, I want to export the shortlist as a PDF so I can share it with my student.
   180→
   181→### Counselor
   182→
   183→- As a counselor, I want to export a filtered list as CSV so I can analyze it in a spreadsheet.
   184→- As a counselor, I want to use the table view with custom columns so I can compare specific metrics across many schools.
   185→- As a counselor, I want to share a search via URL so I can send a pre-filtered view to a student or family.
   186→- As a counselor, I want to use sample searches as starting points for common student profiles.
   187→
   188→### Authenticated User
   189→
   190→- As a returning user, I want to save my home location so distance and My Price are calculated automatically on every visit.
   191→- As a user, I want my column layout and view mode preferences to persist between sessions.
   192→
   193→### AI Client
   194→
   195→- As an AI assistant, I want to query available search fields so I can understand what filters exist.
   196→- As an AI assistant, I want to search colleges with structured filters so I can answer user questions about college options.
   197→
   198→---
   199→
   200→## 5. Data Sources
   201→
   202→| # | Source | Short Name | Data Provided | URL |
   203→|---|---|---|---|---|
   204→| 1 | National Center for Education Statistics (IPEDS) | NCES | Academics, cost, outcomes, enrollment, demographics, retention, graduation rates, faculty ratios, endowments | nces.ed.gov/ipeds |
   205→| 2 | U.S. News & World Report — National Universities | US News | National university rankings | usnews.com |
   206→| 3 | U.S. News & World Report — Liberal Arts Colleges | US News | Liberal arts college rankings | usnews.com |
   207→| 4 | Forbes America's Top Colleges | Forbes | Overall college rankings | forbes.com |
   208→| 5 | Washington Monthly College Rankings | Wash Monthly | Rankings emphasizing social mobility, research, and public service (national + liberal arts) | washingtonmonthly.com |
   209→| 6 | Niche Best Colleges | Niche | Rankings based on academics, value, and student reviews | niche.com |
   210→| 7 | Money Magazine Best Colleges | Money | College value scores balancing quality, affordability, and outcomes | money.com |
   211→| 8 | National Collegiate Athletic Association | NCAA | Division and conference data for intercollegiate athletics | ncaa.org |
   212→| 9 | FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean | 538 | Congressional-district-level partisan lean direction and scores | fivethirtyeight.com |
   213→| 10 | Koppen Climate Classification | Koppen | Climate group, temperature subtype, and precipitation subtype | wikipedia.org (Koppen) |
   214→| 11 | College Application Platforms | Apps | Which platforms each school accepts (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | commonapp.org |
   215→| 12 | U.S. Census Bureau | Census | Metropolitan area names, distances, and IPEDS locale classifications | census.gov |
   216→
   217→---
   218→
   219→## 6. Architecture Overview
   220→
   221→Kapow is a monorepo with a Go API backend, a React frontend, and a PostgreSQL
   222→database. For detailed architecture documentation, see:
   223→

# Kapow — College Power Search: Product Specification

## 1. Product Overview

**Kapow (College Power Search)** is a web application that provides a comprehensive, faceted search engine for US colleges and universities. It aggregates data from 12 independent sources into a single searchable interface, enabling users to filter, sort, compare, and export institution data across 48 searchable dimensions.

### Target Users

- **Prospective students** researching colleges that fit their academic, financial, geographic, and lifestyle preferences
- **Parents and families** comparing cost, outcomes, and value across institutions
- **High school counselors** building filtered shortlists and exporting data for students
- **AI assistants** querying college data programmatically via the MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint

### Core Value Proposition

College Power Search consolidates data that is otherwise scattered across dozens of websites — federal statistics, multiple ranking publications, athletic conferences, climate data, partisan lean scores, and application platform listings — into a single interface with real-time faceted filtering. Users can combine any number of criteria simultaneously, view results in four different formats, create custom ranking blends from multiple sources, and export their findings as CSV or PDF.

---

## 2. Feature Inventory

### 2.1 Faceted Search Engine

The search engine exposes 48 filterable fields organized into 11 active themes (with 7 additional theme slots reserved for future expansion). Every filter is applied server-side as a parameterized SQL WHERE clause against a denormalized materialized view (`dataset_merged`), ensuring fast queries across the full dataset.

**Active themes and their fields:**

| Theme | Fields | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Core | Institution Name (full-text), Unit ID | Name search across institution name, city, and state |
| Location | City, State, Geographic Proximity, Locale, Region, Metro Area, Metro Distance | Where the school is and what kind of setting it occupies |
| Academics | Institution Type, Highest Degree, Enrollment, Graduate Enrollment, Student-Faculty Ratio, Full-Time Faculty Rate, Gender (coed/single-sex), Online Only, Carnegie Classification | Academic profile and size |
| Cost & Financial Aid | Avg Net Price, In-State Tuition, Out-of-State Tuition, Room & Board, Net Price (low-income), Pell Grant Rate, Federal Loan Rate, Endowment | What it costs and how it is funded |
| Outcomes | Admission Rate, Graduation Rate, Retention Rate, Transfer Rate | Student success metrics |
| Rankings | US News National, US News Liberal Arts, Forbes, Washington Monthly National, Washington Monthly Liberal Arts, Niche, Money Score | Rankings from 6 publications (7 lists) |
| Admissions | Application Platforms (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | How to apply |
| Athletics | NCAA Division, NCAA Conference | Athletic conference and competition level |
| Fields of Study | CIP-coded program offerings | What majors and programs are available |
| Cultural Environment | Partisan Direction, Partisan Score, Congressional District | Political leaning of the surrounding area |
| Weather | Koppen Climate Group, Koppen Temperature Subtype, Koppen Precipitation Subtype | Climate classification of the campus location |

**Filter types supported:**

| Filter Type | Behavior | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Full-text search | PostgreSQL `tsvector` matching | "MIT" matches Massachusetts Institute of Technology |
| Substring match | Case-insensitive partial match | "Arbor" matches Ann Arbor |
| Single enum | Exact match from a fixed option list | Institution Type = "Public" |
| Multi enum | Match any or all from a set | State in [CA, NY, MA] |
| Numeric range | Min/max bounds on a numeric value | Tuition between $5,000 and $20,000 |
| Exact string | Case-insensitive exact match | Metro Area = "Boston" |
| Exact numeric | Exact integer match | Carnegie Classification = 15 |
| Geographic proximity | PostGIS radius search | Within 50 miles of a given lat/lng |
| Ranking | Numeric range on ranking position | US News rank 1-25 |

### 2.2 Four Result Views

Users can switch between four ways to view search results:

| View | Description |
|---|---|
| **Card** (default) | Responsive grid of summary cards showing key stats per institution |
| **List** | Compact row-based list emphasizing quick scanning |
| **Table** | Full data table with sortable columns, customizable column visibility, and drag-to-reorder |
| **Map** | Leaflet-based map with institution markers; supports click-to-detail |

The selected view mode persists in `localStorage` and can also be set via URL parameter (`?view=table`).

### 2.3 Custom Ranking Blends

Users can create weighted composite rankings by assigning weights to any combination of the 7 ranking fields. When a blend is active, results are sorted by the computed blend score rather than by a single column. Blend weights are serialized in the URL (`?blend=...`) so blended searches can be shared via link.

### 2.4 Sorting and Pagination

- Any field marked as `sortable` can be used as the sort column (ascending or descending)
- When a user location is set, results can be sorted by distance
- Pagination supports configurable page sizes (5, 10, 20, 50, 100) with a maximum of 100 results per search
- All sort and pagination state is encoded in URL parameters for shareability

### 2.5 Column Customization (Table View)

In table view, users can:
- Show or hide any output field using a column picker
- Reorder columns via drag-and-drop or up/down controls
- Column visibility and order persist in `localStorage`
- Default-visible columns are determined by field metadata from the API

### 2.6 Institution Detail

Clicking any institution in any view opens a detail drawer/modal showing expanded information including:
- Core identity (name, city, state, coordinates)
- Institution type and enrollment
- Admission and graduation rates
- Tuition (in-state and out-of-state) and average net price
- Retention rate and student-faculty ratio

### 2.7 Similar Institutions

The API provides a `similarInstitutions` query that, given a specific institution, returns other schools with similar profiles based on type, enrollment, admission rate, graduation rate, and tuition. Results include a similarity distance score.

### 2.8 CSV and PDF Export

- **CSV Export**: Server-side export via `/api/export/csv` that respects active filters, sort order, and user location. Downloads directly as a file. Includes all exportable fields.
- **PDF Export**: Client-side PDF generation using the current result set, active output fields, and active filter labels. Includes data source attribution.

### 2.9 Geographic Features

- **Proximity search**: PostGIS-powered `ST_DWithin` queries with configurable radius (up to ~310 miles / 500 km)
- **Distance calculation**: When a user location is set, each result includes its distance from that location
- **My Price**: When a user's home state is known, the system returns in-state tuition for public schools in that state and out-of-state tuition otherwise
- **Map view**: Interactive Leaflet map with markers for all results in the current page
- **ZIP code resolution**: The `resolveZip` query resolves a US ZIP code to latitude/longitude and state, used by the `LocationInput` component for location-by-ZIP entry (queries `location.zip_coordinates`)

### 2.10 Authentication and User Preferences

- **Authentication**: Supabase-based authentication supporting Google, GitHub, and email sign-in via OAuth
- **User profile**: The `me` query returns the authenticated user's ID, email, display name, avatar, and auth provider
- **Preferences**: Authenticated users can save a home location (latitude, longitude, state) that automatically populates the user location for distance calculations and My Price computation

### 2.11 Sample Searches

The home page features a carousel of pre-built sample searches that demonstrate the system's filtering capabilities:

| Sample | Filters Applied |
|---|---|
| Top 25 National | US News National rank <= 25 |
| Affordable in California | State = CA, net price <= $20,000 |
| D1 Small Schools | NCAA Division I, enrollment <= 5,000 |
| Selective & Affordable | Admission rate <= 30%, net price <= $20,000 |
| D3 New England | NCAA Division III, New England region |
| Sunny & Affordable | Dry (Koppen B) climate, net price <= $15,000 |

Each sample search shows a live preview of matching institutions before the user navigates to the full search page.

### 2.12 MCP (Model Context Protocol) Endpoint

The application exposes a WebMCP interface that allows AI clients (such as Claude) to programmatically search the college database. Two tools are registered:

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `search_colleges` | Execute a filtered search with field/value pairs, limit, and offset |
| `get_search_fields` | Retrieve the full field descriptor catalog including types and options |

### 2.13 Data Source Attribution

Every search field is linked to its originating data source. The UI can display source badges and tooltips, and the `dataSources` GraphQL query returns the full catalog with names, short names, URLs, and descriptions. This attribution carries through to PDF exports.

---

## 3. Pages

| Route | Page | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | Home | Landing page with hero search form (name, state, ranking, major), sample search carousel, and feature highlights |
| `/search` | Search | Main search interface with collapsible filter sidebar (desktop: fixed 280px sidebar; mobile: toggle-to-show), results area with view mode toggle, export buttons, pagination, and institution detail drawer |
| `/about` | About | Information page about the application |
| `/auth/callback` | Auth Callback | OAuth redirect handler for Supabase authentication |

All pages share a common `Header` (with navigation and auth controls) and `Footer`.

---

## 4. User Stories

### Student

- As a student, I want to search for colleges by name so I can quickly find a specific school.
- As a student, I want to filter by state, cost, and admission rate simultaneously so I can find affordable schools I have a realistic chance of attending.
- As a student, I want to filter by fields of study so I can find schools that offer my intended major.
- As a student, I want to see how far each school is from my home so I can gauge travel time.
- As a student, I want to view results on a map so I can see the geographic distribution of matching schools.
- As a student, I want to create a custom ranking blend weighting cost and graduation rate so I can find the best value schools by my own criteria.

### Parent

- As a parent, I want to compare in-state and out-of-state tuition so I can understand the true cost.
- As a parent, I want to see my personalized net price (My Price) so I know what public schools in my state would actually cost.
- As a parent, I want to sort by graduation rate so I can prioritize schools where students succeed.
- As a parent, I want to export the shortlist as a PDF so I can share it with my student.

### Counselor

- As a counselor, I want to export a filtered list as CSV so I can analyze it in a spreadsheet.
- As a counselor, I want to use the table view with custom columns so I can compare specific metrics across many schools.
- As a counselor, I want to share a search via URL so I can send a pre-filtered view to a student or family.
- As a counselor, I want to use sample searches as starting points for common student profiles.

### Authenticated User

- As a returning user, I want to save my home location so distance and My Price are calculated automatically on every visit.
- As a user, I want my column layout and view mode preferences to persist between sessions.

### AI Client

- As an AI assistant, I want to query available search fields so I can understand what filters exist.
- As an AI assistant, I want to search colleges with structured filters so I can answer user questions about college options.

---

## 5. Data Sources

| # | Source | Short Name | Data Provided | URL |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | National Center for Education Statistics (IPEDS) | NCES | Academics, cost, outcomes, enrollment, demographics, retention, graduation rates, faculty ratios, endowments | nces.ed.gov/ipeds |
| 2 | U.S. News & World Report — National Universities | US News | National university rankings | usnews.com |
| 3 | U.S. News & World Report — Liberal Arts Colleges | US News | Liberal arts college rankings | usnews.com |
| 4 | Forbes America's Top Colleges | Forbes | Overall college rankings | forbes.com |
| 5 | Washington Monthly College Rankings | Wash Monthly | Rankings emphasizing social mobility, research, and public service (national + liberal arts) | washingtonmonthly.com |
| 6 | Niche Best Colleges | Niche | Rankings based on academics, value, and student reviews | niche.com |
| 7 | Money Magazine Best Colleges | Money | College value scores balancing quality, affordability, and outcomes | money.com |
| 8 | National Collegiate Athletic Association | NCAA | Division and conference data for intercollegiate athletics | ncaa.org |
| 9 | FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean | 538 | Congressional-district-level partisan lean direction and scores | fivethirtyeight.com |
| 10 | Koppen Climate Classification | Koppen | Climate group, temperature subtype, and precipitation subtype | wikipedia.org (Koppen) |
| 11 | College Application Platforms | Apps | Which platforms each school accepts (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | commonapp.org |
| 12 | U.S. Census Bureau | Census | Metropolitan area names, distances, and IPEDS locale classifications | census.gov |

---

## 6. Architecture Overview

Kapow is a monorepo with a Go API backend, a React frontend, and a PostgreSQL
database. For detailed architecture documentation, see:

- **[Frontend Architecture](frontend-architecture.md)** — tech stack, components, state management, GraphQL integration
- **[GraphQL API](graphql-api.md)** — queries, mutations, types, request lifecycle
- **[Search Engine](search-engine.md)** — field types, SQL generation, themes, data sources
- **[Data Model](data-model.md)** — schemas, tables, relationships, migrations
- **[Authentication](authentication.md)** — JWT flow, middleware, Supabase integration
- **[Deployment](deployment.md)** — Docker images, Compose, env vars, startup sequence

python3 -c "
import json
with open('contracts/product-contracts.json') as f:
    contracts = json.load(f)
print(f'Loaded {len(contracts)} contracts')
with open('contracts/schema/product-contract.schema.json') as f:
    schema = json.load(f)
required = schema['required']
statuses = schema['properties']['status']['enum']
categories = schema['properties']['category']['enum']
for c in contracts:
    for r in required:
        assert r in c, f'{c[\"id\"]} missing required field: {r}'
    assert c['status'] in statuses, f'{c[\"id\"]} invalid status: {c[\"status\"]}'
    assert c['category'] in categories, f'{c[\"id\"]} invalid category: {c[\"category\"]}'
    import re
    assert re.match(r'^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$', c['id']), f'{c[\"id\"]} invalid id format'
print('All contracts valid against schema')
for c in contracts:
    print(f'  {c[\"status\"]:12s} {c[\"id\"]}')" 2>&1
